_meta.note: This status file records the structural audit findings. Re-run tools/check-progression-integrity.mjs inside the repo to refresh source-shape counts.

summary.verdict: The progression pipeline has clear authority seams and several fail-closed checks, but runtime safety is not fully proven until Foundry smoke testing covers skipped/applicability-filtered steps, Force choice grants, rollback, and droid/nonheroic edge cases.
strongSignals: Step base class defines a clean plugin contract for selection, validation, rendering, and commit behavior.
Node registry is the candidate-node SSOT and includes mode/subtype/dependency/invalidation metadata.
Node-to-plugin map is centralized and explicit.
ActiveStepComputer derives active steps from the node registry, applicability, and subtype adapter contribution.
ProgressionFinalizer compiles from canonical progressionSession.draftSelections and routes mutations through ActorEngine.
Level-up manifest is read-only and finalization validates required choices against it.
Droid forbidden selections are validated before mutation.
primaryRisks: ActiveStepComputer hides non-applicable steps. If applicability logic is wrong, a required step can vanish before the user can choose it.
Step plugins still maintain local committed state and hydrate from draftSelections, so backtracking and rapid-click behavior need runtime stress testing.
Finalizer validation is strong for level-up choices, but chargen validation remains coarser and primarily checks class, attributes, credits, and droid state.
Force technique/secret/regimen pipelines are structurally present but choice entitlement, repeatable selection, and item materialization need runtime verification.
Subtype adapters can suppress or modify active steps after the canonical applicability pass, creating a second structural decision point that needs audit/smoke coverage.
Single-step finalization has a separate compile path from full progression finalization, so parity must be tested separately.

notes: The base plugin requires getSelection, onItemCommitted, validate, and getBlockingIssues for concrete steps.
Major choice steps hydrate from progressionSession.draftSelections and commit through _commitNormalized or explicit draft updates.
Survey/review/summary/null-style steps should be treated separately because they may be display or confirmation surfaces rather than item-choice producers.
Runtime verification should focus on backtracking, rapid clicking, and whether local step state exactly mirrors canonical draftSelections after render cycles.
Level-up entitlement manifest is the read-only receiving dock for one level-up event.
Finalizer validates level-up required selections with validateLevelUpRequiredSelections before mutation.
Class features that are choices are counted; materialized automatic features become locked class-feature items.

matrix.finalization: indirect through selected class validity; runtime verify
required count audited, per-feat legality should be runtime verified
required count audited, per-talent legality should be runtime verified

notes: Finalizer validates readiness, subtype adapter readiness, document type, mutation plan shape, and then applies through ActorEngine.
The finalizer explicitly acknowledges ActorEngine failure may occur after partial mutation; this should be part of rollback smoke testing.
Post-finalization level-up audit checks class level, selected items, automatic features, and class skills.
Force technique step resolves entitlements through force-suite-resolution and builds pending class-grant state for prerequisite evaluation.
Force Power Mastery is treated as a choice-bearing repeatable technique with special identity handling.
Jedi Knight, Jedi Master, Sith Apprentice/Lord, Force Adept, and Force Disciple should be runtime-smoked because these are the highest-risk class-grant/Force-choice intersections.
Droid builder is species-equivalent for droids; finalizer intentionally skips biological species materialization for droid progression.
Droid forbidden selections are blocked before mutation.
Nonheroic has a dedicated starting feats step and is listed as a subtype across the registry; runtime verify that heroic-only progression assumptions do not leak into this flow.
Follower/beast subtypes appear in node coverage, but they need manual smoke coverage because adapter behavior can change active steps after registry filtering.

recommendedBatches: Batch 1: Runtime smoke and small parity fixes for step applicability vs finalizer required-choice validation.
Batch 2: Force progression hardening for technique/secret/regimen repeatable choices and detail-rail hydration.
Batch 3: Droid/nonheroic/follower edge-case hardening and adapter-specific active-step tests.
Batch 4: Rollback and single-step-vs-full-finalizer parity hardening.
Batch 5: Optional strict mode for check-progression-integrity once baseline findings are accepted.
runtimeChecklist: Level 1 chargen for each base class.
Multiclass into a second base class.
Attempt illegal prestige class and confirm commit/finalization block.
Jedi Knight gains and applies a Force Technique.
Jedi Master, Sith Lord, and Force Disciple gain/apply Force Secrets.
Force Training selects powers and updates Force suite counts.
Species with natural weapon creates a usable weapon/attack.
Rapid-click through mentor dialogue and step confirmations.
Roll attack, damage, and skill after finalization to confirm derived recalculation.
